purpose: Canonical scientific-claims matrix for public project wording.

status_legend.implemented: Implemented in the codebase, but not externally validated as valuable.
status_legend.controlled_evidence: Supported by controlled benchmark evidence under a narrow protocol.
status_legend.strong_intra_theory_evidence: Strongly supported inside the theory the implementation operationalizes.
status_legend.early_controlled_evidence: Initial comparative or task evidence exists, but remains narrow or early.
status_legend.not_established: Not supported by current public evidence.
status_legend.falsified: Tested under pre-registered protocol and result was negative; no further retry planned under the current design.
status_legend.retry_planned: Tested under pre-registered protocol and result was negative; a targeted follow-up study is planned at higher power or on a more suitable dataset.

oracle_affect_note: requires_oracle_affect=true: the claim's primary positive-result benchmarks inject preset valence/arousal at encode time (oracle affect); the LLM/keyword appraisal pipeline is bypassed for those results. requires_oracle_affect=false: the benchmark ran without preset affect injection — either no affect signal (naturalistic) or the appraisal pipeline itself produced the signal.

claims.claim_text: AFT is implemented as a coherent multi-layer memory engine.
claims.allowed_public_wording: AFT is implemented as a coherent multi-layer memory engine.
claims.current_evidence: Public API, engine/retrieval/state modules, sync/async parity tests.
claims.next_study: Keep API/docs aligned while external evaluations grow.

claims.current_evidence: 127 fidelity cases validate affect-aware ranking logic. The controlled quadrant benchmark (SBERT) ties AFT and naive_cosine at recall@5 ≈ 0.80-0.85 (ceiling effect, N = 20 items) while clearly beating recency (Δ = −0.55, p < 0.001). SOTA extension on affect_reference_v1 (2026-05-07, gpt-4.1-mini): Mem0 reaches recall@5 = 1.00 (Δ = +0.20 vs cosine, p_boot = 0.045) and LangMem 0.90 (Δ = +0.10, p_boot = 0.254); AFT 0.85 (Δ = +0.05, p_boot = 0.825). LLM-backed memory dominates AFT on that simpler probe but at 25× higher encode latency (Mem0 1130 ms/item vs AFT 45 ms/item). The realistic replay v2 benchmark (Hd2 PASS) shows AFT top1 = 0.53 vs naive_cosine = 0.33 with SBERT (N = 200; Δ=+0.205 [0.150,0.265], p_bootstrap<0.001, d=0.49). SOTA replication on v2 (2026-05-07, gpt-4.1-mini, H_v2_sota PASS): on the same v2 dataset Mem0 = 0.330 (Δ vs cosine = +0.005, p=0.945) and LangMem = 0.365 (Δ = +0.040, p=0.233); neither beats naive_cosine. AFT top1 = 0.535 (Δ vs cosine = +0.210 [+0.155,+0.270], p<0.001, d=0.512) — non-overlapping CIs vs both SOTA systems. Pattern inverts across benchmarks: Mem0 excels at semantic quadrant recall (affect_reference_v1) while AFT excels at affect-modulated multi-session recall (v2). Architecture advantage under automatic appraisal: Hg1 FAIL (LLM appraisal + oracle-free; Δ=−0.010, p=0.367). All Hd* results use oracle/preset affect. — FR realistic_recall_v2 (Addendum M, 2026-05-16, multilingual-e5-small, Branch A PASS): N=120 queries × 30 native-FR hand-authored scenarios; AFT top1=0.31 [0.23, 0.39], naive_cosine top1=0.12 [0.07, 0.18], Δ=+0.18 [0.11, 0.26], p_bootstrap=0.0000, Hedges g=0.424. Advantage confirmed in French on same 2-session realistic replay format as EN.
claims.not_yet_shown: General downstream superiority over production memory systems in the semantic-recall regime — Mem0 and LangMem outperform AFT on affect_reference_v1 (simple quadrant probe). Architecture advantage under automatic appraisal unconfirmed (Hg1 FAIL). Consistent cross-embedder per-type advantage (H6 momentum_alignment fails for e5-small-v2). Cross-language replication on IT/ES at me5 N=120 in 2-session format remains open (Hd2 Branch C FAIL 2026-05-07); FR is the sole 2-session confirmatory cross-language PASS (Addendum M, me5, N=120). Cross-domain ecological replication: Hk1 FAIL on DailyDialog (Branch B, Δ=-0.008, p_holm=1.000, 2026-05-07). Human / ecological validation (#27 open).
claims.next_study: Robust cross-language replication (explicit confirmatory study); ecological corpus with >30% affect-bearing turns per dialogue for naturalistic replication. Inter-rater agreement (κ Cohen) on affect_reference_v1.

claims.claim_text: On multi-session realistic replay (v2), AFT (oracle-affect) outperforms production LLM-backed memory systems (Mem0, LangMem) on top1_accuracy.
claims.allowed_public_wording: On the realistic multi-session replay benchmark, AFT with preset affect labels achieves top1_accuracy = 0.535 vs Mem0 = 0.330 and LangMem = 0.365 — an advantage of Δ +0.21 (d=0.51) with non-overlapping 95% CIs. Neither LLM-backed system outperforms naive cosine on this benchmark.
claims.current_evidence: H_v2_sota PASS (exploratory, 2026-05-07, gpt-4.1-mini, sbert-bge, N=200, n_bootstrap=10000, seed=0): AFT top1=0.535 [0.465,0.600], Mem0=0.330 [0.265,0.395], LangMem=0.365 [0.300,0.430], cosine=0.325 [0.260,0.390]. Pairwise vs cosine: AFT Δ=+0.210 [+0.155,+0.270], p<0.001, d=0.512; Mem0 Δ=+0.005, p=0.945; LangMem Δ=+0.040, p=0.233. Per-challenge AFT advantage strongest on semantic_confound (+0.275), momentum_alignment (+0.275), affective_arc (+0.275). Pattern inverts vs affect_reference_v1 (where Mem0=1.00>AFT=0.85): the two benchmarks test different capabilities. Methodological asymmetry: AFT receives oracle affect; Mem0/LangMem do not.
claims.not_yet_shown: Advantage without oracle-affect (Hg1 FAIL). Advantage on factual QA (LoCoMo S1 FAIL). Advantage under realistic appraisal. Human evaluation.
claims.next_study: SOTA replication on v2 with automatic appraisal (would require gpt-4.1-mini appraisal, consistent with Hg1 methodology). Cross-embedder replication (e5-small-v2 on v2 SOTA).

claims.claim_text: The implementation is faithful to the theories it operationalizes.
claims.allowed_public_wording: The implementation is faithful to the theories it operationalizes.
claims.current_evidence: 127 fidelity cases across 20 phenomena validate expected intra-theory behavior. S3 ablation (N=200, SBERT and e5-small-v2) covers all 7 AFT mechanisms: individual no_mood/no_resonance effects are not significant in the expected direction, dual_path and synchronous keyword variants are destructive, and no_reconsolidation remains null on this benchmark. The 8-variant ablation shows system-level advantage but no isolatable single-layer attribution. Hi3 PASS (Addendum I closure, 2026-05-06, N=500, seed=1): the per-challenge embedder amplification on semantic_confound is confirmed — e5 resonance interference exceeds SBERT by Δ=+0.090 [0.030,0.160] (d=0.257, Holm-adj p=0.0234). Secondary Hi3_recency also PASS (Δ=+0.070, p_adj=0.0234); Hi3_arc FAIL (Δ=+0.010). Mechanism channel (Hi2: link-set density) not differentiated at link-count level — both embedders saturate top-5 cap.
claims.not_yet_shown: Ecological correspondence to human emotional memory. The LLM-appraisal dual-path advantage on affect-free data is falsified (Addendum G/P, see appraisal_llm_real_dual_path). Mechanism for embedder-dependent amplification not localised at link-count instrumentation level (Hi2 still exploratory).
claims.next_study: Human and behavioral validation. The automatic-appraisal dual-path question is closed (Addendum G/P, falsified).

claims.current_evidence: The appraisal-quality benchmark provides early controlled evidence on natural-language inputs.
claims.not_yet_shown: Calibration across models, domains, and languages.
claims.next_study: Appraisal robustness study across models, domains, and languages.

claims.claim_text: AFT helps on replayable multi-session memory tasks.
claims.allowed_public_wording: AFT helps on replayable multi-session memory tasks (v2, N=200, SBERT: AFT top1=0.53 vs naive_cosine=0.33, Δ=+0.21 [0.15,0.27], p<0.001; e5-small-v2: AFT top1=0.50 vs 0.34, Δ=+0.16 [0.09,0.22], p<0.001).
claims.current_evidence: Realistic replay v2 (50 scenarios, 200 queries, 5 challenge types × 40). SBERT bge-small-en: AFT top1=0.53 vs naive_cosine=0.33, Δ=+0.205 [0.150,0.265], p_bootstrap<0.001, d=0.49. e5-small-v2: AFT top1=0.50 vs naive_cosine=0.34, Δ=+0.155 [0.090,0.225], p_bootstrap<0.001, d=0.31. Advantage holds on both embedder classes. Architecture attribution confirmed: Hd1 PASS (Addendum D, seed=1, 2026-04-27); Gate 3 CLOSED. — FR realistic_recall_v2 (Addendum M, 2026-05-16, multilingual-e5-small, Branch A PASS): N=120 queries × 30 native-FR scenarios (2-session multi-turn); AFT top1=0.31 [0.23, 0.39], naive_cosine=0.12 [0.07, 0.18], Δ=+0.18 [0.11, 0.26], p_bootstrap=0.0000, Hedges g=0.424.
claims.not_yet_shown: General superiority on external open-domain QA (LoCoMo Gate 1 FAIL — AFT F1=0.168 vs naive_rag=0.271) or completed human/ecological validation. Generalization beyond affect-discriminative scenarios: v2 benchmark has 4/5 challenge types where AFT signals are theoretically relevant by design; advantage on valence-neutral tasks is not established.
claims.next_study: Execute human-evaluation pilot. Per-task retrieval-weight tuning closed by Hj1 FAIL (Add. J).

claims.claim_text: AFT does not outperform naive RAG on external open-domain conversational QA (LoCoMo).
claims.allowed_public_wording: On the LoCoMo conversational QA benchmark (1986 QA pairs, 10 conversations), AFT retrieval underperforms a naive RAG baseline (F1 0.168 vs 0.271; judge_acc 0.279 vs 0.441). Gate 1 was not met. AFT's affective weighting does not help on factual open-domain QA.
claims.current_evidence: Pre-registered S1 run completed 2026-04-27. H1 and H2 both fail (Δ<0). Holm-corrected one-tailed p=1.0 for both hypotheses. Add. J Pareto sweep executed 2026-05-06 (10 weight configs × 200-QA stratified subsample, seed=42, 50 per category). Hj1 FAIL: no AFT config achieves F1 ≥ naive_rag on any category. Best AFT config (W2: sem=0.50, mood=0.30, no resonance) achieves aggregate F1=0.1765 vs naive_rag F1=0.2092 (Δ=−0.033); closest cell W2×multi_hop Δ=−0.011. The per-task base_weights tuning line is closed as honest negative. Addendum L (query-type routing, 2026-05-19): closed-loop heuristic routing on LoCoMo 200-QA smoke test. Hl1 FAIL (Δ=−0.017, below +0.02 threshold and in wrong direction). Hl2 FAIL (Δ=−0.081 vs naive_rag). Hl3 data-collection issue (classifier log bug). Branch B verdict: routing ships as optional feature; claim status unchanged.
claims.not_yet_shown: Whether selective affect suppression on literal queries or factual/affective dual branching could close the gap. base_weights tuning alone cannot match naive_rag (Hj1 FAIL, Add. J closure). Closed-loop query-type routing also FAIL (Addendum L, Hl1 Branch B).
claims.next_study: Architectural approaches beyond weight tuning: selective affect suppression, factual/affective dual-branch retrieval, or hybrid systems.

claims.claim_text: Resonance interference is amplified under e5-small-v2 vs SBERT on semantic_confound queries.
claims.allowed_public_wording: Hi3 PASS (N=500, seed=1): e5-small-v2 shows larger resonance interference than SBERT on semantic_confound queries (Δ=+0.090 [0.030,0.160], d=0.257, Holm-adj p=0.0234). The v2 per-challenge finding (post-hoc Δ=+0.100) is confirmed as not a sample-size artefact.
claims.current_evidence: Hi3 confirmatory study (Addendum I closure, 2026-05-06): paired bootstrap n=10,000 seed=1, Holm m=3. Primary Hi3 (semantic_confound): Δ=+0.090, CI=[0.030,0.160], p_one=0.0078, p_adj=0.0234, d=0.257, PASS. Secondary Hi3_recency (recency_confound): Δ=+0.070, p_adj=0.0234, d=0.239, PASS. Hi3_arc (affective_arc): Δ=+0.010, p_adj=0.3795, FAIL. Dataset: realistic_recall_v3.json (N=500, affect-rich).
claims.not_yet_shown: Mechanism for amplification: link-count instrumentation shows both embedders saturate top-5 cap (mean≈5.0); link-type or link-strength differentiation not yet measured. Counterfactual (raising ResonanceConfig.cosine_threshold) not executed.
claims.next_study: Link-type/strength instrumentation to localise the Hi2 spreading-activation over-fire hypothesis.

claims.claim_text: Resonance interference amplification under e5 vs SBERT extends to recency_confound queries.
claims.allowed_public_wording: Hi3_recency PASS (secondary, Holm-corrected): e5 resonance interference exceeds SBERT on recency_confound queries (Δ=+0.070 [0.020,0.130], d=0.239, Holm-adj p=0.0234, N=500).
claims.current_evidence: Hi3_recency: paired bootstrap n=10,000 seed=1. Δ=+0.070, CI=[0.020,0.130], p_one=0.0112, p_adj=0.0234, d=0.239, PASS. Secondary hypothesis in Holm m=3 family; reported descriptively.
claims.next_study: Part of Hi3 family — follow-up contingent on mechanism study.

claims.claim_text: Resonance interference amplification under e5 vs SBERT on affective_arc queries.
claims.allowed_public_wording: Hi3_arc FAIL: no significant amplification on affective_arc queries (Δ=+0.010 [-0.020,0.050], d=0.058, Holm-adj p=0.3795, N=500). The embedder gap does not extend to affective_arc at this sample size.
claims.current_evidence: Hi3_arc: paired bootstrap n=10,000 seed=1. Δ=+0.010, CI=[-0.020,0.050], p_one=0.3795, p_adj=0.3795, d=0.058, FAIL. Holm secondary; honest negative result.
claims.not_yet_shown: Whether a larger N or different dataset would show signal.
claims.next_study: No active follow-up planned; Hi3_arc FAIL scopes the amplification claim to semantic/recency channels.

claims.claim_text: AFT with LLM dual-path appraisal outperforms naive cosine on affect-free data.
claims.allowed_public_wording: Hg1 FAIL (twice): AFT with LLMAppraisalEngine (dual-path, gpt-5-mini) does not outperform naive cosine on affect-free queries. Original Hg1 (realistic_recall_v3_noAF, N=200): delta=-0.010, p=0.367. Re-run with the recalibrated SEC->affect mapping on a leakage-free set disjoint from the calibration data (Addendum P, realistic_recall_v4_noAF, N=160): delta=-0.087 [-0.144,-0.031], p=0.0018, d=-0.242 -- naive cosine significantly ahead. The LLM affect signal is real (beats neutral, Hp2 PASS) and the dual-path schedule is essential (beats synchronous, Hp3 PASS, d=0.95), but the full system still trails pure cosine when queries are affect-free. The oracle-affect circularity remains the scope boundary of the Hd1/Hd2 claims.
claims.current_evidence: Addendum G closure 2026-05-07: realistic_recall_v3_noAF (50 scenarios, 200 queries), sbert-bge embedder, gpt-5-mini LLM appraisal. aft_llm_dual top1=0.315 [0.250,0.380] vs naive_cosine top1=0.325 [0.260,0.390]. Δ=-0.010, d=-0.032, p_one=0.3669. Exploratory: Hg2 FAIL (aft_llm_dual vs aft_neutral Δ=0.000); Hg3 PASS (aft_llm_dual vs aft_llm_sync Δ=+0.185, d=0.342). Diagnosis refined (WP-1a, 2026-05-30, gpt-5-mini, N=750 on realistic_recall_v3 oracle): the appraisal signal is correlated but mis-calibrated, not absent — valence Pearson r=0.81, sign accuracy=0.86, systematic valence bias +0.19 and arousal bias -0.14. A pre-registered prompt-calibration attempt (Addendum N, N=150) failed both hypotheses: valence bias dropped to +0.044 (Hn1 requires both axes; arousal unchanged at -0.118) and gold-set regressed 1/15; the prompt change was reverted. Addendum O (mapping recalibration, 2026-05-31, gpt-5-mini, N=750 SEC dump, by-scenario 70/30 split seed 42) numerically recalibrated the SEC→valence/arousal projection on the train scenarios and promoted model M1 (Scherer feature basis preserved; valence intercept constrained to 0). Held-out test (228 events): valence bias +0.200→+0.072, arousal bias -0.144→-0.023; MAE improved on both axes (valence 0.306→0.298, arousal 0.182→0.118), Pearson r within the -0.05 guardrail. Both pre-registered hypotheses (Ho1 bias, Ho2 guardrail) PASS; M1 weights are now live in `_scherer_project`. Addendum P (Hg1 re-run, 2026-05-31, gpt-5-mini, sbert-bge, seed 0, n_bootstrap 10000) executed the pre-registered re-run with the recalibrated M1 mapping on a leakage-free affect-free dataset DISJOINT from realistic_recall_v3 (realistic_recall_v4_noAF, 40 scenarios / 160 queries, pNN_ namespace, zero overlap on scenario/memory ids and event text, frozen before the run): aft_llm_dual top1=0.800 [0.738,0.863] vs naive_cosine top1=0.887 [0.838,0.931], Hp1 delta=-0.0875 [-0.144,-0.031], one-tailed p=0.0018, d=-0.242 -- FAIL, naive cosine SIGNIFICANTLY ahead (gap widened vs Hg1's -0.010 and crossed into significance). Recalibration did not rescue affect-free retrieval. Exploratory: Hp2 dual>neutral PASS (0.800 vs 0.744, delta=+0.056, p=0.030) -- the LLM affect carries signal; Hp3 dual>sync PASS (0.800 vs 0.287, delta=+0.512, d=0.95) -- the deferred dual-path schedule is essential (synchronous appraisal collapses). Net: a better-calibrated affect signal is a net distractor on affect-free queries even though it is not noise; the architecture-vs-cosine delta stays falsified.
claims.not_yet_shown: Nothing further pre-registered for the affect-free architecture-vs-cosine comparison: the recalibrated mapping (Addendum O M1) does NOT change the Hg1 verdict (Addendum P, leakage-free). The LLM dual-path advantage remains scoped to oracle-affect conditions (Hd1/Hd2). The Hg3/Hp3 PASS (dual-path > synchronous) is reaffirmed. Mapping weights are calibrated for gpt-5-mini's appraisal output; a different appraisal engine may need re-fitting.
claims.next_study: DONE. Addendum P re-ran Hg1 on a leakage-free dataset disjoint from v3 with the recalibrated M1 mapping: Hp1 FAIL (naive cosine significantly ahead). No further architecture-vs-cosine study planned; the claim is falsified for affect-free retrieval.

claims.claim_text: AFT affect-conditioned retrieval advantage generalises to ecological dialogue corpora.
claims.allowed_public_wording: Hk1 FAIL (Branch B, 2026-05-07): DailyDialog ecological replication — AFT does not outperform naive cosine on naturalistic short-turn dialogue (N=120 personas, 396 queries, multilingual-e5-small; Δ=-0.008, p_holm=1.000). Addendum M FR PASS (Branch A, 2026-05-16): native French scenarios, 2-session realistic replay (N=120, me5); AFT top1=0.31 vs naive_cosine=0.12, Δ=+0.18 [0.11, 0.26], p<0.0001, Hedges g=0.424.
claims.current_evidence: Hk1 pre-registered and executed 2026-05-07. Synthetic personas from DailyDialog train+validation (12118 dialogs, 2580 after ≥30% emotion-density filter). 4 programmatic query types from Ekman labels (no LLM). Paired bootstrap n=10000 seed=0, Holm m=4. Aggregate: AFT top1=0.212 vs naive_cosine=0.220 (Δ=-0.008, CI=[-0.056,+0.043], p_holm=1.000, d=-0.015). Only affective_trajectory shows positive trend (Δ=+0.103, d=0.186, N=39) but underpowered (p_holm=0.734). All directional query types FAIL Holm threshold. Branch B confirmed. — FR realistic_recall_v2 (Addendum M, 2026-05-16, multilingual-e5-small, Branch A PASS): N=120 queries × 30 native-FR hand-authored scenarios; AFT top1=0.31 [0.23, 0.39], naive_cosine top1=0.12 [0.07, 0.18], Δ=+0.18 [0.11, 0.26], p_bootstrap=0.0000, p_McNemar=0.0000, Hedges g=0.424. Effect concentrated on same_topic_distractor (top1=0.42) and affective_arc (top1=0.38); momentum_alignment remains weak (top1=0.12). Prior expectation was FAIL; evidence diverges from prior without post-hoc reframing.
claims.not_yet_shown: Cross-domain replication on an affect-rich naturalistic corpus (>30% affect-bearing turns). Whether longer multi-turn sessions with richer emotion vocabulary would show signal consistent with affective_trajectory trend (d=0.186). Cross-language replication in non-Romance languages. IT/ES cross-language replication in 2-session realistic replay format (current IT/ES data uses appraisal_confound single-session runner).
claims.next_study: Retry targeted affective_trajectory sub-claim at N≥120 on a naturalistic corpus with >30% affect-bearing turns (DailyDialog affect density too low). Pre-register before executing. Gate 2 human eval (kit ready, zero ratings — v1.0 scope).

claims.claim_text: The system models human emotional memory.
claims.allowed_public_wording: The system is theory-inspired, but does not yet have human or ecological validation.
claims.next_study: Gate 2 (human eval): kit ready (benchmarks/human_eval/), zero ratings collected. Tracking continues on roadmap v1.0; does not block the v0.11.x line.
